findings.title: Complete todo can erase the still-unproven non-empty swap-hook-data obligation.
evidence: Trigger: a downstream planner scans open todos for remaining V4 hook-data fork work.
Path: todo 007 is marked status: complete while its own checked acceptance criterion says the residual non-empty swap-hook-data proof is either satisfied or separately tracked.
Observation: grep found the residual proof only in prose in this completed todo, the brainstorm, and the implemented plan; no new open todo or machine-readable blocker artifact tracks it.
Failure: the original non-empty fork/harness proof can disappear from active work even though current artifacts correctly report nonEmptySwapHookData=false.
findings.recommendation: Before keeping status complete, create/link a new explicit follow-up for production or local-harness non-empty swap hookData proof, or mark 007 as policy/catalog-complete but residual-proof-open.

findings.title: Manual coverage flags can overclaim V4 hook behavior without a decoded-payload invariant catching it.
evidence: Trigger: a route author edits candidate capabilities or copies a basedflick route to another V4 route.
Path: v4HookAddress and v4NonEmptyHookData are manual candidate flags; generateGapMatrix copies route.capabilities directly into fork-facing rows.

Current tests spot-check fame-to-weth and weth-to-fame, and Foundry only counts actual non-empty hookData keys; it does not assert every route and matrix capability matches decoded V4 payload hook address and hookData length.
Failure: a regenerated artifact could report v4NonEmptyHookData=true or v4HookAddress=true for a route whose V4 payload has empty hookData or no hook address, while hash/generation checks still pass.
findings.recommendation: Derive V4 coverage flags from compiled legs/pool metadata, or add an invariant test over every route and every generated matrix row comparing capabilities to decoded V4 payloads.

findings.title: Catalog evidence labels are static references, so catalog proof can drift away from actual fork-covered routes.
evidence: Trigger: basedflick/ZORA remains in CREATOR_COIN_POOL_SPECS but is later removed from solver routes or stops executing on the pinned fork.

Path: the catalog still emits proves.hookAddressSwap=true and evidence type pinned-fork-metadata-validation from static strings; catalog tests assert entry shape and hookData policy, not a routeArtifactId or fork matrix linkage.
Failure: the catalog can look like executable hook-address proof even when it has become metadata-only evidence.
findings.recommendation: Add routeArtifactIds/forkEvidenceRouteIds to catalog entries and assert each proving entry is referenced by at least one executable fork-tested matrix row, or rename proves.* to metadataCoverage.*.

residual_risks: The artifact layer now separates hook-address and non-empty hookData claims, but the Base fork suite was not run in this review, so live execution of regenerated composed routes was not re-confirmed here.

The current catalog starts from committed config, not onchain fleet-style import/discovery; that is acceptable for a deterministic baseline but does not yet reduce hand-maintained pool metadata risk as much as a real importer would.
testing_gaps: Missing invariant: for every generated route, decode each V4 payload and assert v4HookAddress equals nonzero hooks and v4NonEmptyHookData equals payload.hookData.length > 0.
Missing invariant: every gap-matrix row with routeArtifactId must have capabilities identical to the referenced route artifact.
Missing invariant: every catalog entry claiming hookAddressSwap proof must link to at least one generated route/fork matrix row that uses the same poolId.
Not run in this review: Doppler-backed pinned Base fork route execution.
